seoTitle: How to Change Paper Size in Google Docs (2026)
metaDescription: Switch between Letter, A4, and Legal page sizes in Google Docs using Page Setup, and set your preferred size as the account default.
intro: Preparing an international contract or a local print document often requires a different page size than Google Docs' default. This guide covers switching between Letter, A4, and Legal presets through Page Setup, and locking in your preferred size for future documents.

steps.heading: Open Page Setup
steps.body: Click File in the top menu and scroll down to select Page Setup, which opens the layout configuration dialog.

steps.heading: Choose Pages or Pageless view
steps.body: Decide whether you want a traditional paginated layout or the newer pageless format, depending on your document's purpose.

steps.heading: Select a paper size preset
steps.body: Click the Paper Size dropdown and choose a standard option like A4 or Legal to match your target region or use case.

steps.heading: Adjust margins if needed
steps.body: Modify the margin fields so your text and tables still align properly with the new page dimensions.

steps.heading: Set as default for future documents
steps.body: Click 'Set as default' before confirming, then click OK to apply the changes to your current document.

faq.question: Will changing paper size affect existing text placement?
faq.answer: Yes, text and images can shift slightly to accommodate the new page boundaries, so a quick review afterward is recommended.

faq.question: Can I set a completely custom page size?
faq.answer: Google Docs offers standard presets, though some custom dimensions may be available depending on your account type.

faq.question: Does this setting apply to every document I create?
faq.answer: Only if you click 'Set as default'; otherwise the change applies just to the current file.

faq.question: What's the difference between Letter and A4?
faq.answer: Letter is the standard US paper size, while A4 is the international standard used in most other countries.
